_source: VeraData /sanctions LIVE production response (real entity, name undisclosed for privacy), provided by teodorofodocrispin-cmyk in x402-foundation/x402#2749 (2026-07-01T17:22:02Z)
_distinction_from_sanctions_response_cle: That fixture is a synthetic conformance-test entity where we have query_hash_input from scratch, so query_hash itself is independently recomputable. This one is a real production call over an undisclosed real entity — query_hash is taken as given (their input, correctly not disclosed), and only the event_hash/chain_hash chain from that point forward is independently verified. Both properties matter and are complementary: full-construction recompute (the other fixture) and real-production-chain-integrity (this one).

recompute_construction.note: query_hash is TAKEN AS GIVEN here (the real screened entity's name is not disclosed, correctly). What's independently verified: event_hash = SHA256('sha256:' + query_hash + '|0.0000|' + checked_at + '|' + policy_ref), and chain_hash = SHA256('sha256:' + genesis + '|sha256:' + event_hash) where genesis = SHA256('veradata-aat-genesis-v1'). Both independently recomputed and confirmed matching by babyblueviper1, same day.
recompute_construction.verified_via_review: Also run through the real invinoveritas /review endpoint (artifact_type=sanctions_screening): verdict=approve, confidence=0.95, decision_ref=sha256:0a13c127fb21b96325ccec1bddcae4300f5dc7cf388150b506a398054229afbe. Deterministic overclaim-boundary check found one real, minor gap: lists_checked present (honest scope) but no explicit matches field — VeraData confirmed fixing this (adding matches: [] to all CLEAN responses).

expected.overclaim_boundary_note: Missing matches field at time of this fixture — a real, live example of the exact boundary the overclaim check exists to catch, not a hypothetical.
